title: Fix delete post and reply — dialog closes without deleting

requirements.summary: Fix the delete post mutation in VideoPostCard so that confirming the Delete dialog actually calls the backend deleteVideoPost function, awaits the result, and invalidates/removes the post from the query cache. The dialog must only close after the deletion succeeds.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Delete in the Delete Post confirmation dialog removes the post from the backend.
The post disappears from the feed/UI immediately after the dialog closes.
If the delete call fails, the dialog stays open or shows an error; the post is not removed from the UI.

file_operations.description: Fix the handleDelete function to properly await the deleteVideoPost mutation before closing the AlertDialog. Ensure the delete confirmation button is disabled during the mutation, handle errors gracefully by keeping the dialog open and displaying an error message, and only close the dialog after successful deletion. Add loading state to the Delete button to provide user feedback during the async operation.

requirements.summary: Fix the delete reply mutation in VideoReplyCard so that confirming the Delete dialog actually calls the backend deleteVideoReply function, awaits the result, and invalidates/removes the reply from the query cache. The dialog must only close after the deletion succeeds.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Delete in the Delete Reply confirmation dialog removes the reply from the backend.
The reply disappears from the UI immediately after the dialog closes.
If the delete call fails, the dialog stays open or shows an error; the reply is not removed from the UI.

file_operations.description: Fix the handleDelete function to properly await the deleteVideoReply mutation before closing the AlertDialog. Ensure the delete confirmation button is disabled during the mutation, handle errors gracefully by keeping the dialog open and displaying an error message, and only close the dialog after successful deletion. Add loading state to the Delete button to provide user feedback during the async operation.

requirements.summary: Audit the useDeleteVideoPost and useDeleteVideoReply mutation hooks in useQueries.ts to ensure they correctly reference the backend actor methods (e.g., actor.deleteVideoPost / actor.deleteVideoReply), pass the correct post/reply ID argument, and call queryClient.invalidateQueries or optimistically remove the item on success.
acceptanceCriteria: The mutation hooks call the correct backend actor methods with the correct ID.
On mutation success, affected query caches are invalidated so the deleted item no longer appears.
No silent failures — errors from the backend surface as mutation errors.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the useDeleteVideoPost and useDeleteVideoReply mutation hooks. Ensure they correctly call actor.deleteVideoPost(postId) and actor.deleteVideoReply(replyId) respectively, passing bigint IDs correctly. Verify that mutationFn properly awaits the backend call and that onSuccess handlers call queryClient.invalidateQueries(['videoPosts']) for posts and queryClient.invalidateQueries(['videoReplies', parentVideoId]) for replies. Add proper error handling so mutation errors propagate to the UI instead of failing silently.
